item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this SCP; just have a few nitpicks.

+ Show
- Hide

identified several unidentifiable anomalies

I know what you're trying to say, but using the exact same word twice makes it sound like a contradictory statement.

UN Spacy

Why Spacy? I thought it was a typo at first, but it appears more than once… Am I missing something? For me, it just took me out of the story.

Also, I changed "attemtps" to "attempts"

item.title: Xyank continues
item.content:encoded: Great work, integration between multiple scp brings the world together. Can't wait for more Xyank story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent writing, and I love the Macross reference. +1, would space again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is extremely underrated. I love the world building and the exquisitely bizarre anomaly itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh yeah, it sure was. They (the books at least, I haven't seen the show) get space so right that it's hard not to be inspired by it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was this inspired by the Expanse at all?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, all I can say is that I sure had an objective and purpose in mind. Unfortunately I buried the led a bit too deep. I get why it's a bit too a tragedy and out of left field for many to follow. Perhaps I'll come back and revisit, but it's hard.

My vision was that no one ought to be able to understand it. It seems I have succeeded beyond my wildest dreams.

I respect your decision. You want some insight, drop me a pm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What I love about this is the worldbuilding, which is an incredible amount of fun. Seriously, just a joy. There were many other small details that I quite enjoy.

What I don't love about this is the anomaly, pretty much at all. I see upthread that you're trying to represent an alien intelligence beyond our understanding, which is a laudable goal. It's one of my favorite ideas in fiction. Unfortunately, I mostly just ended up not following anything at all. I primarily say this about the D-Class transmission logs, since most of the rest could be seen as build-up. With so much time dickery and cross-cutting and random events, I couldn't pick up any thread, it just felt like utter randomness.

Utter randomness isn't satisfying or interesting. Part of the fun of the vast alien intelligence story is when you as a reader begin to figure out that that's what's up. I may be rare in this, but I never found those cracks.

Without something else behind it, the anomalous zone and God-craft felt like the set-up (but none of the payoff) for an original series Star Trek episode. Without more payoff, the cross-links feel cheap and hack-y. (Also, the picture looks rather fake.)

No vote, I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like a wibblier wobblier version of the Friendly Vacuum. I really enjoy hard sci-fi anomalies when they're executed right (as per SCP-2460, SCP-2474 et al.) and this is a very good "true alien" intelligence in the vein of the latter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To be more specific:

  • Naturally occurring phenomenon
  • Self containing
  • Beneficial to upholding normalcy OR of a nature that extensive containment would impede normalcy
  • Low informational security needs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Occam class huh? For things that are weird, but really let's not bother containing them it's fine? :p

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes sense. I think, if nothing else, you've conveyed that this isn't just some random weird thing doing random weird stuff: there's a definite pattern to the events, just not one that really makes any sense. And that's what makes this work. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So they found the fifthism starfish, it seems like. Neat! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey hey, glad to see this got posted, and with some pretty kick ass pics none the less.

Like I said during review, its fitting that you include the mention to 2001: A Space Odyssey, since you really capture that same vibe here well, without being a straight up knock off. Additionally, its refreshing to see yet another far future skip on the site, this time with a colonized Mars, and a young Xyank before the time travel shenanigans went down.

+1. Good show.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I specifically left it's purpose a bit ambiguous. I mean, I know what it's doing, but it's supposed to be beyond our reckoning altogether.

I was inspired by a reddit post discussing the prospect of dealing with a seriously advanced aline intellect. Think of a highway from the pov of an ant. Ant has no idea that what it's crawling on or across is man made. It doesn't even know what man made means. That's the level of difference in intellect that's going on here. It's not malicious, but we don't know what it is or what it's doing. And maybe can't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll never get tired of hard sci-fi deep space skips that explicitly take place in the future. :3

So what I get out of this is it's trying to figure out what we are, while making stuff we might like and a place we can live. What does it know that we don't? :O

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Denizens of 19 and several channels which shall not be named helped me with this little beauty here, bringing it up to snuff and clearing out the dust. Special consideration to salvagebar, Roget, ARD, and probably several others I'm forgetting.

Image credits!

Everything used is either public domain or on a CC license compatible with SCP-Foundation site use. The originals can be found here, here, and here.

Enjoy.
